_meta.null_signal_reason: No new developments in coverage window; persistent EU AI Act Standards Vacuum confirmed as standing signal

module_0.title: EU AI Act Standards Vacuum persists ahead of high-risk obligations
module_0.body: No harmonised standards published in Official Journal as of Q1 2026, with high-risk AI obligations due August 2026. CEN-CENELEC drafts in progress but compliance gap remains active, creating regulatory uncertainty for providers. GPAI Code of Practice first release published, due for full finalisation Q2 2026. Watch for CEN-CENELEC updates and potential enforcement delays.

mainstream.headline: EU AI Act Standards Vacuum active pre-high-risk deadline
mainstream.why_it_matters: Ongoing absence of Official Journal standards complicates compliance for high-risk systems due August 2026, risking fragmented national implementations and enforcement challenges across Member States.

mainstream.headline: GPAI Code of Practice first release published
mainstream.why_it_matters: Initial version provides interim guidance for general-purpose AI models, with full finalisation pending Q2 2026, shaping transparency and systemic risk obligations for frontier providers.

mainstream.headline: EU AI Act entered force August 2024 with phased rollout
mainstream.why_it_matters: Provisions apply gradually through 2026-2027, establishing risk-based framework as global benchmark despite implementation frictions.

underweighted.headline: CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 drafts underway without OJ publication
underweighted.why_it_matters: Standards development lags essential requirements timeline, enabling definitional arbitrage for non-standard-compliant systems and pressuring EU AI Office coordination.

underweighted.headline: EDPS designated as MSA for EU institutions AI systems
underweighted.why_it_matters: Expands oversight to internal EU bodies, creating dual role as notified body for high-risk conformity, but risks resource constraints in multi-level supervision.

european.item: Standards Vacuum active ahead of high-risk obligations
european.summary: No harmonised standards in Official Journal, CEN-CENELEC drafts in progress for high-risk requirements due August 2026. Gap triggers compliance uncertainty for providers placing systems on EU market.
european.asymmetric: Creates arbitrage for GPAI models below compute thresholds while high-risk classifiers face enforcement variability.

european.item: GPAI Code of Practice first release published
european.summary: Interim code advances transparency for general-purpose models, full version due Q2 2026 ahead of 12-month obligations.
european.asymmetric: Pressures non-EU labs for extraterritorial compliance, testing AI Acts global reach.

european.item: EDPS as market surveillance authority for EU institutions
european.summary: EDPS oversees AI systems in EU bodies and handles high-risk conformity assessments.
european.asymmetric: Internalises enforcement for institutional use, potentially accelerating precedent for private sector.

vectors.summary: No harmonised standards in Official Journal ahead of high-risk obligations August 2026.
vectors.asymmetric: Enables market entry via national workarounds, fragmenting single market goals.

vectors.summary: Phased rollout strains multi-level supervision with AI Office and national authorities.
vectors.asymmetric: GPAI extraterritoriality tests enforcement against US labs.

vectors.summary: Risk-based categories create gaps for GPAI and open-source models.
vectors.asymmetric: Sparse architectures may evade compute thresholds.

vectors.summary: National MSAs and EU AI Board face coordination challenges.
vectors.asymmetric: Resource gaps in EDPS dual role amplify institutional risks.

vectors.summary: Sandboxes mitigate but high-risk rules burden SMEs.
vectors.asymmetric: Regulatory burden shifts power to compliant incumbents.

vectors.summary: AI Act extraterritoriality collides with US/China frameworks.
vectors.asymmetric: Sets precedent for standards contest in ISO/IEC.

law_highlights.title: EU AI Act prohibits unacceptable risk systems from February 2025
law_highlights.summary: Bans social scoring and predictive policing, enforced via market surveillance.
law_highlights.asymmetric: Extraterritorial scope targets non-EU providers with EU users.

standards_highlights.title: CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 developing technical standards
standards_highlights.summary: Drafts detail essential requirements but not yet in Official Journal.

governance_gaps.analysis: Absence of OJ standards precedes high-risk deadlines.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: Risks inconsistent conformity assessments across states.

structural_trends.title: Risk-based AI categorisation
structural_trends.trend: Prohibited and high-risk systems reshape societal deployments

domains.note: Centralised oversight via Board and Office

governance_health_composite.note: Standards vacuum drags composite; GPAI code progress offsets marginally

key_judgments.judgment: EU AI Act Standards Vacuum enables compliance arbitrage until August 2026

weekly_brief_draft: The persistent EU AI Act Standards Vacuum defines this weeks signal, with no harmonised standards in the Official Journal ahead of high-risk obligations in August 2026. CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 drafts progress slowly, leaving providers in a compliance limbo that risks fragmented enforcement across Member States and definitional gaps for GPAI models. The first release of the GPAI Code of Practice offers interim transparency guidance, set for full finalisation in Q2 2026, but does little to close the vacuum for high-risk systems. EDPS designation as market surveillance authority for EU institutions adds internal oversight capacity, yet highlights resource strains in the multi-level system comprising national MSAs, the AI Office, and the European AI Board. Risk indicators hold steady, with Standards Vacuum at HIGH due to arbitrage potential, Regulatory Implementation Lag at ELEVATED from coordination challenges, and Global Fragmentation at HIGH as extraterritorial rules test US labs. No new model releases, investments, or science breakthroughs register, underscoring a quiet week focused on regulatory plumbing. Sector penetration remains structural, with prohibited practices banned since February 2025 reshaping societal uses. Power concentrates in EU bodies like the AI Office, while lab postures show Anthropic leading in proactive engagement as GPAI signatory. Governance health composite at 0.45 reflects standards drag, stable absent acute shifts. Jurisdiction matrix flags EU at ELEVATED risk from vacuum, US at HIGH dependency without binding rules. Key judgment: vacuum enables arbitrage until deadline. Watch CEN-CENELEC milestones and GPAI systemic-risk designations for capability-governance friction.

cross_monitor_candidates.signal: Standards Vacuum risks EU tech sovereignty in high-risk compliance
Deployment gaps from vacuum may inflate inefficient AI energy use
